supporting_persona_summaries.main_thesis: The market is broadly right that Yes is the base case because Binance ETH/USDT is comfortably above 2200, but 94.5% looks slightly too high for a venue-specific one-minute crypto close that is still nearly a day away.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market prior of 94.5% Yes.
Check the governing source of truth and exact settlement mechanics on Polymarket rules.
Verify current Binance ETH/USDT level and short-horizon context directly on Binance data.
Conclude that Yes is the base case, but trim confidence modestly for tail-risk and venue-specific settlement mechanics.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance ETHUSDT checks were around 2352.5-2352.6, well above 2200.
Binance 24h low in the check was 2308.5, still above the threshold.
strongest_disconfirmers: Crypto can still move more than 6% in less than a day.
Settlement is venue-specific and minute-specific, so a Binance-only wick or operational anomaly could matter.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether Binance website chart presentation and public API would ever diverge in a way that matters for settlement.
How much tail-risk should be charged over the remaining sub-24h window.
supporting_persona_summaries.what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if Binance ETH/USDT moved materially closer to 2200, if Binance underperformed other spot references, or if an exchange-specific operational/data issue appeared near settlement.
supporting_persona_summaries.timing_relevance: Very high: the market resolves on the Apr 17 12:00 ET Binance 1-minute candle close, and the remaining window is short enough that current spot matters but long enough for crypto tail-risk to remain live.
supporting_persona_summaries.source_quality_view: High-quality direct venue data plus clear contract rules, with only medium independence because most important evidence points back to the same underlying Binance market.
